member.name: T:Wss.CalibrationModule.IStimParamsCore
member.summary: Core + parameters surface layered over . Provides normalized-drive stimulation, per-channel parameter access, and JSON persistence for stimulation parameters. BASIC is optional and can be exposed via .

member.name: M:Wss.CalibrationModule.IStimParamsCore.StimulateNormalized(System.Int32,System.Single)
member.summary: Computes a pulse width from a normalized value in [0,1] using per-channel parameters (minPW, maxPW, amp, IPI) and forwards the result to the core. Implementations clamp the input to [0,1] and cache the last PW sent.

member.name: M:Wss.CalibrationModule.IStimParamsCore.GetStimIntensity(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the most recently computed stimulation intensity for the channel. For PW-driven systems, this is the last pulse width (µs) sent by .
member.returns: Most recent intensity value (µs or mA depending on mode).

member.name: M:Wss.CalibrationModule.IStimParamsCore.SaveParamsJson
member.summary: Saves the current stimulation-parameters JSON to disk.

member.summary: Loads the stimulation-parameters JSON from a specific file path or directory.

member.name: T:Wss.CoreModule.ContactConfigDefinition
member.summary: Describes a contact configuration to create on the WSS device.

remarks.para: The setup arrays use caller-facing ordering where index 0 is closest to the switch and index 3 is farthest. Each element must be 0 (unused), 1 (source), or 2 (sink).
LED configuration is optional and may be ignored by firmware that does not support LED settings.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.ContactConfigDefinition.ContactId
member.summary: Gets or sets the contact configuration ID (0..255).

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.ContactConfigDefinition.StimSetup
member.summary: Gets or sets the stimulation-phase contact roles. Provide exactly 4 values where index 0 is closest to the switch.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.ContactConfigDefinition.RechargeSetup
member.summary: Gets or sets the recharge-phase contact roles. Provide exactly 4 values where index 0 is closest to the switch.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.ContactConfigDefinition.Leds
member.summary: Gets or sets the optional LED bitmask. Leave null to omit LED configuration from the command payload.

member.name: T:Wss.CoreModule.CoreConfig
member.summary: Represents the core configuration persisted to stimConfig.json. Includes device-count, firmware, target-address, and amplitude-curve settings.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.CoreConfig.maxWSS
member.summary: Maximum number of WSS devices supported by this app.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.CoreConfig.firmware
member.summary: Firmware version string (e.g., "H03", "J03").

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.CoreConfig.broadcastTarget
member.summary: Gets or sets the on-wire broadcast receiver address used when sending to all devices. The value is persisted as a hexadecimal string such as 0x8F.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.CoreConfig.wssTargets
member.summary: Gets or sets the on-wire receiver addresses for logical Wss1, Wss2, and Wss3. Index 0 maps to Wss1, index 1 to Wss2, and index 2 to Wss3. Each entry is persisted as a hexadecimal string such as 0x81.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.CoreConfig.useConfigAmpCurves
member.summary: When true, use per-WSS amplitude curve parameters from this config. When false, use built-in defaults (72mA) or device-reported mode (10mA) mapping.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.CoreConfig.ampCurves
member.summary: Per-WSS amplitude curve parameters. Index 0 => Wss1, 1 => Wss2, 2 => Wss3. Defaults to three entries matching the 72mA built-in curve.

member.name: T:Wss.CoreModule.AmpCurveParams
member.summary: Piecewise amplitude curve parameters used to map mA to 0..255. mA <= LowThreshold: out = pow(mA / LowConst, ExpPower) + 1 mA > LowThreshold: out = ((mA + LinearOffset) / LinearSlope) + 1

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.AmpCurveParams.LowThreshold
member.summary: Boundary between the two segments in mA.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.AmpCurveParams.LowConst
member.summary: Low-range divisor constant (C) for the power segment.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.AmpCurveParams.ExpPower
member.summary: Exponent used in the low-range power segment (already reciprocal, e.g., 1/1.5466).

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.AmpCurveParams.LinearOffset
member.summary: Offset used in the high-range linear segment.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.AmpCurveParams.LinearSlope
member.summary: Slope divisor used in the high-range linear segment.

member.name: M:Wss.CoreModule.AmpCurveParams.Default72mA
member.summary: Returns the built-in 72 mA piecewise curve parameters that match the historical mapping used by the core (power segment below 4 mA and linear segment above).

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.CoreConfigController._configPath
member.summary: Resolved file path to the configuration JSON.

member.summary: Initializes a controller pointing to a custom path. If a directory path is provided, "stimConfig.json" is created inside it.
member.param: Absolute or relative file path, or a directory path.
member.exception: Thrown when is null, empty, or whitespace.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.CoreConfigController.IsLoaded
member.summary: Indicates whether the JSON configuration has been loaded into memory.

member.name: M:Wss.CoreModule.CoreConfigController.LoadJson
member.summary: Loads the configuration from disk. Creates and saves a default config if missing or invalid. Safe to call multiple times.

member.name: M:Wss.CoreModule.CoreConfigController.LoadJson(System.String)
member.summary: Loads the core configuration from disk at the specified . If is a directory, the file name stimConfig.json is appended. If the file does not exist or is invalid, a default configuration is created and saved. Safe to call multiple times; the operation is thread-safe.
member.param: Absolute or relative file path, or a directory path ending with a path separator.

member.name: M:Wss.CoreModule.CoreConfigController.SaveJson
member.summary: Threads safe, persists the current configuration to disk. No-op if not loaded yet.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.CoreConfigController.MaxWss
member.summary: Gets max number of WSS supported by this app. Thread safe get.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.CoreConfigController.Firmware
member.summary: Gets the firmware version saved on config. Thread safe get.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.CoreConfigController.BroadcastTarget
member.summary: Gets the configured on-wire broadcast receiver address.
member.remarks: The value is read from the persisted hexadecimal string in . If the stored value is missing or invalid, the historical default 0x8F is returned and the normalized value is immediately persisted back to disk.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.CoreConfigController.WssTargets
member.summary: Gets the configured on-wire receiver addresses for logical Wss1..Wss3. A normalized three-entry copy is returned.
member.remarks: Missing entries are backfilled with the historical defaults 0x81, 0x82, and 0x83. When normalization changes the in-memory configuration, the updated values are immediately persisted.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.CoreConfigController.UseConfigAmpCurves
member.summary: When true, the core should use per-WSS amplitude curves from the config file.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.CoreConfigController.AmpCurves
member.summary: Returns the configured amplitude curve parameters per WSS (index 0..2 maps to Wss1..Wss3).
member.remarks: The returned array is the live in-memory configuration array, not a defensive copy. Mutating the returned array or its entries changes the loaded configuration until it is reloaded. Call to persist those changes.

member.name: M:Wss.CoreModule.CoreConfigController.SetMaxWss(System.Int32)
member.summary: Updates the maximum number of supported WSS devices in the loaded configuration and immediately persists the change to disk. Thread safe.
member.param: New maximum WSS count. Must be greater than zero.
member.exception: Thrown when is less than or equal to zero.

member.name: M:Wss.CoreModule.CoreConfigController.SetFirmware(System.String,Wss.CoreModule.WSSVersionHandler)
member.summary: Updates the firmware version recorded in the configuration and immediately saves it to disk. Thread safe.
member.param: Firmware version string to record (for example, "H03").
Version handler used to validate whether the supplied firmware string is supported.
member.exception: Thrown when is null.
Thrown when is not supported by .

member.remarks: This request supports the same payload shapes as the previous overload set: basic event, event with explicit shape IDs, event with amplitude and pulse-width data, or event with both shape and amplitude data. and must be supplied together. and must be supplied together. is required when amplitude arrays are supplied, and amplitude arrays are required when is supplied.

member.name: T:Wss.CoreModule.IFrameCodec
member.summary: Contract for framing and deframing messages over byte-stream transports (Serial, BLE, TCP).

member.name: M:Wss.CoreModule.IFrameCodec.Frame(System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte[])
member.summary: Builds a wire-safe frame for transmission using SLIP-style escaping. Output format (before escaping): [sender][target][payload...][checksum][END].
member.param: Target/receiver address byte (e.g., device address or broadcast).
The protocol payload (e.g., [cmd][len][...]). The codec does not modify the payload.
member.returns: Escaped frame ready to send (always terminates with END).

member.name: M:Wss.CoreModule.IFrameCodec.Deframe(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Progressive deframer: feed any-size incoming chunks and get back zero or more complete, unescaped frames. Each returned frame has the format [sender][target][payload...][checksum] (END is consumed).
member.param: A raw byte chunk from the transport (may be partial or contain multiple frames).
member.returns: 0..N complete, unescaped frames (END removed). Checksum is not validated here.

member.name: M:Wss.CoreModule.IStimulationCore.Tick

member.name: M:Wss.CoreModule.IStimulationCore.Shutdown

member.name: T:Wss.CoreModule.ITransport
member.summary: Abstraction over a byte-stream transport (e.g., Serial, Bluetooth, TCP). Implementations surface incoming bytes via and provide async connect/disconnect/send operations.

remarks.para: This interface is intentionally minimal and transport-agnostic so higher-level code can be reused with different physical links (UART/COM, RFCOMM, BLE GATT, sockets).
Threading: Implementations typically raise from a background thread. If your application requires main-thread access, marshal the callback to the main thread.
Chunking: Incoming chunks may contain partial frames, multiple frames, or arbitrary boundaries. Always pass chunks to a frame codec/deframer instead of assuming message boundaries align with reads.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.ITransport.IsConnected
member.summary: Gets whether the transport is currently connected/open.

member.name: E:Wss.CoreModule.ITransport.BytesReceived
member.summary: Raised whenever raw bytes arrive from the underlying transport. Each invocation may represent any number of bytes (including zero, depending on implementation), and may contain partial or multiple protocol frames.

remarks.para: Implementations should invoke this event promptly as data becomes available. Callers should treat the provided buffer as read-only and copy it if they need to retain it.
This event is typically raised on a background thread. Consumers should marshal to the main thread if required by their environment.

member.name: M:Wss.CoreModule.ITransport.ConnectAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Opens/initializes the transport connection (e.g., open a serial port, connect a socket, start BLE session).
member.param: Optional cancellation token to abort the connect attempt.
member.returns: A task that completes when the transport is connected.
member.exception: If the instance has been disposed.
If is canceled.
Implementations may throw transport-specific exceptions (e.g., unauthorized port access, device not found).

member.name: M:Wss.CoreModule.ITransport.DisconnectAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Closes the transport connection and stops any background receive loop.
member.param: Optional cancellation token to abort a long-running disconnect.
member.returns: A task that completes when the transport is fully disconnected.
member.exception: If the instance has been disposed.
If is canceled.

member.name: M:Wss.CoreModule.ITransport.SendAsync(System.Byte[],System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Sends a block of bytes over the transport.
member.param: The bytes to write. Callers should provide a buffer that will not be mutated during send.
Optional cancellation token to cancel the send before it is written. For some transports (e.g., synchronous serial writes), cancellation may only be observed before the write starts.
member.returns: A task that completes when the bytes have been handed off to the transport.
member.exception: If the instance has been disposed.
If the transport is not connected.
If is canceled.

member.name: T:Wss.CoreModule.ModuleSettings

member.name: T:Wss.CoreModule.ModuleSettings.HwConfigFlags
member.summary: Hardware configuration flags reported by the device. Each bit defines an independent setting; the on-wire value is the sum of set bits. In particular, bit 1 (value 2) indicates 10 mA PA mode and bit 2 (value 4) indicates 10 ms pulse guard.

member.name: F:Wss.CoreModule.ModuleSettings.HwConfigFlags.TenMilliAmpPA

member.name: F:Wss.CoreModule.ModuleSettings.HwConfigFlags.TenMsPulseGuard
member.summary: Enables 10 ms pulse guard (bit 2; value 4).

member.name: F:Wss.CoreModule.ModuleSettings.HwConfigFlags.Future8

member.name: T:Wss.CoreModule.TestModeTransport
member.summary: In-memory transport for debugging and unit tests. No real I/O. Features: artificial latency, jitter, inbound chunking, and drop simulation. Default auto-responder validates checksum, flips sender/target, and echoes or replaces payload.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.TestModeTransport.BaseLatency
member.summary: Artificial latency applied to inbound delivery.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.TestModeTransport.JitterMs
member.summary: Random jitter added to BaseLatency, in milliseconds.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.TestModeTransport.MaxInboundChunkSize
member.summary: If >0, splits inbound payloads into chunks of up to this size.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.TestModeTransport.InboundDropProbability
member.summary: Probability [0..1] of randomly dropping an inbound chunk.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.TestModeTransport.Rng
member.summary: Random generator used for jitter, chunk sizing, drops, and synthetic replies.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.TestModeTransport.FallbackPayload
member.summary: Payload used when incoming checksum is invalid.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.TestModeTransport.AutoResponderAsync
member.summary: Optional override auto-responder. If null, the built-in default responder is used.

member.name: M:Wss.CoreModule.TestModeTransport.#ctor(Wss.CoreModule.TestModeTransportOptions)
member.summary: Initializes a new with a single options object.
member.exception: Thrown when is null.
member.remarks: Null and values are replaced with safe defaults.

member.name: M:Wss.CoreModule.TestModeTransport.ConnectAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Connects the transport. No real I/O occurs, only state change.

member.name: M:Wss.CoreModule.TestModeTransport.DisconnectAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Disconnects the transport and cancels any pending operations.

member.name: M:Wss.CoreModule.TestModeTransport.SendAsync(System.Byte[],System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.remarks: The default auto-responder will generate a reply based on checksum validation and invoke .

member.name: M:Wss.CoreModule.TestModeTransport.EnqueueIncoming(System.Byte[],System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Injects inbound bytes as if received from a physical device. They are subject to latency, jitter, chunking, and drop settings before being delivered.

member.name: M:Wss.CoreModule.TestModeTransport.Dispose
member.summary: Disposes the transport, cancels background operations, and releases resources.

member.name: T:Wss.CoreModule.TestModeTransportOptions
member.summary: Holds behavior settings for .
member.remarks: The transport constructor requires a non-null options instance. When or is left null, substitutes safe defaults.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.TestModeTransportOptions.BaseLatency
member.summary: Gets or sets the artificial latency applied to inbound delivery.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.TestModeTransportOptions.JitterMs
member.summary: Gets or sets the random jitter added to , in milliseconds.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.TestModeTransportOptions.MaxInboundChunkSize
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum inbound chunk size. Values greater than zero split inbound payloads into chunks up to this size.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.TestModeTransportOptions.InboundDropProbability
member.summary: Gets or sets the probability in the range [0..1] of randomly dropping an inbound chunk.

member.name: T:Wss.CoreModule.WssClient
member.summary: Low-level protocol client. Frames commands using an , sends them over an , and correlates responses by (target,msgId).

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.WssClient.Started
member.summary: Indicates whether the WSS client connection has been started.

member.name: M:Wss.CoreModule.WssClient.#ctor(Wss.CoreModule.ITransport,Wss.CoreModule.IFrameCodec,Wss.CoreModule.WSSVersionHandler,Wss.CoreModule.WssClientOptions)
member.summary: Creates a low-level protocol client over a caller-provided transport and frame codec.
member.param: Underlying byte transport (for example, serial or BLE).
Frame codec used for escaping/deframing and checksum validation.
Firmware version handler used for version-gated commands.
Client options including sender address, transport ownership, reply timeout, and logical target address mapping.
member.exception: Thrown when , , , or is null.
Thrown when the configured response timeout is not positive.
member.remarks: may be raised on a background thread depending on the transport implementation. Reply correlation primarily matches responses by (target,msgId), with additional handling for protocol error replies and firmware paths that answer using .

member.exception: Thrown if already connected to the target.
Thrown if the connection attempt fails.
Thrown if the operation is canceled.

member.summary: Sends a previously framed command and awaits a single response for the specified (target,msgId) key.
member.param: Completion source to complete when a matching frame arrives.
Fully framed bytes to send (already includes cmd/len and escaping).
Key used to correlate the response: (target,msgId).
Cancellation token. The configured response timeout is also applied.
member.returns: Processed response string.
member.exception: Thrown if is null.
Thrown if the underlying connection encounters an error.
Thrown if the operation is canceled.

member.name: M:Wss.CoreModule.WssClient.Clear(System.Int32,Wss.CoreModule.WssTarget,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Clears groups of resources (0x40): 0=All, 1=Events, 2=Schedules, 3=Contacts.

member.name: M:Wss.CoreModule.WssClient.ModuleQuery(System.Int32,Wss.CoreModule.WssTarget,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Gets settings or information for the target WSS (opcode 0x01). Version-gated: on firmware that supports ModuleQuery (version J+), this sends the command and the reply is cached as data-only bytes per target. On older firmware, no command is sent; for index 1 (settings array) a default 16-byte payload is seeded in the cache so higher layers can decode default unit settings.
member.remarks: - When supported (J+): this method transmits the request and, upon reply, ProcessFrame caches the data-only slice under the sender's target address. Use to retrieve the last snapshot. - When not supported: this method does not transmit. For moduleIndex == 1, it seeds a default 16-byte zero array into the per-target cache, allowing the core to decode a default profile. The returned string indicates that defaults are being used.
member.returns: A human-readable string summary. Cache side-effects are described above.

member.name: M:Wss.CoreModule.WssClient.RequestConfigs(System.Int32,System.Int32,Wss.CoreModule.WssTarget,System.Threading.CancellationToken)

ID associated with the requested configuration (0–255).
Target WSS device to query (default: Wss1).
member.returns: Processed response string from the WSS target.

member.name: M:Wss.CoreModule.WssClient.StreamChange(Wss.CoreModule.StreamChangeRequest,Wss.CoreModule.WssTarget,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Streams changes to PA (amplitude), PW (pulse width), and IPI (period) for up to three schedules in one shot. The grouped request may omit at most one of the three data groups. Uses opcodes: 0x30 = PA+PW+IPI, 0x31 = PA+PW (no IPI), 0x32 = PA+IPI (no PW), 0x33 = PW+IPI (no PA). All elements are 0..255 in this streaming variant (one byte each).
member.param: Target WSS; streaming commonly uses Broadcast.
member.returns: A task representing the outbound streaming send.
member.exception: Thrown when is null.
Thrown when more than one data group is omitted.
Thrown when a supplied group does not contain exactly 3 elements.

member.name: T:Wss.CoreModule.WssClientOptions
member.summary: Holds behavioral settings for .
member.remarks: The constructor requires a non-null options instance and validates to ensure it is greater than zero. Address settings control how logical targets map to on-wire receiver addresses.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.WssClientOptions.Sender
member.summary: Gets or sets the sender address byte placed in outbound frames. The default is 0x00.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.WssClientOptions.OwnsTransport
member.summary: Gets or sets whether disposing the client also disposes the underlying transport. The default is true.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.WssClientOptions.BroadcastTarget
member.summary: Gets or sets the on-wire broadcast receiver address used for logical . The default is 0x8F.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.WssClientOptions.WssTargets
member.summary: Gets or sets the on-wire receiver addresses for logical , , and .
member.remarks: Index 0 maps to , index 1 to , and index 2 to . When null or shorter than three entries, the client falls back to the historical defaults for missing values.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.WssClientOptions.ResponseTimeout
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum time to wait for a correlated reply before canceling the request. This must be greater than zero. The default is 2 seconds.

member.name: T:Wss.CoreModule.WssFrameCodec
member.summary:
Frame codec implementing a SLIP-like protocol:
END (0xC0) terminates a frame.
ESC (0xDB) escapes special bytes inside the frame.
END_SUB (0xDC) represents END when escaped.
ESC_SUB (0xDD) represents ESC when escaped.
Frames on the wire are [sender][target][payload...][checksum][END]. The checksum is computed over all bytes except the last two (checksum + END).

member.name: M:Wss.CoreModule.WssFrameCodec.TryUnescapeAndValidate(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte},System.Byte[]@)
member.summary: Attempts to unescape and validate a single frame (without END), returning false if the checksum does not match. Useful if you want checksum enforcement at the codec layer.
member.param: Escaped frame bytes (without the END terminator).
On success, the unescaped frame [sender][target][payload...][checksum]. On failure, is returned.
member.returns: true if the checksum is valid; otherwise false.

member.name: M:Wss.CoreModule.WssFrameCodec.Unescape(System.Collections.Generic.List{System.Byte})
member.summary: Unescapes an accumulated frame (without END) into raw bytes. Does not validate checksum; see .

member.name: M:Wss.CoreModule.WssFrameCodec.ComputeChecksum(System.Byte[],System.Int32)
member.summary: Computes the WSS checksum by summing all included bytes, folding to 8 bits, and XOR'ing with 0xFF. The last bytes are excluded.
member.param: Buffer that contains the frame data.
Number of trailing bytes to exclude from the sum. Use 2 for outbound frames ([checksum][END]), 1 for inbound frames ([checksum]).

member.remarks: The core owns the lifetime of and disposes it when the core is disposed. Call to stop streaming/disconnect without disposing the transport.

member.name: M:Wss.CoreModule.WssStimulationCore.TryGetModuleSettings(Wss.CoreModule.WssTarget,Wss.CoreModule.ModuleSettings@)
member.summary: Tries to obtain the last decoded ModuleSettings for a target by reading the client's cached ModuleQuery data and decoding it. Returns false if no data is cached or the payload is incomplete; when false, 'settings' may still be non-null but marked as partial.

member.name: T:Wss.CoreModule.WssStimulationCoreOptions
member.summary: Holds startup and default stimulation settings for .
member.remarks: These values affect configuration loading, setup retry behavior, and the default schedule/event values used during the core's initial device setup. Packet spacing is intentionally not exposed here because it remains a fixed hardware/radio constraint in the core implementation. The core constructor requires a non-empty and validates that is at least 1.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.WssStimulationCoreOptions.ConfigPath
member.summary: Gets or sets the core configuration file path. Directory paths resolve to stimConfig.json in that directory.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.WssStimulationCoreOptions.MaxSetupTries
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of setup retries before the core enters the Error state. This must be at least 1. The default is 5.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.WssStimulationCoreOptions.DefaultIpi
member.summary: Gets or sets the default inter-pulse interval used during initial setup, in milliseconds. The default is 10.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.WssStimulationCoreOptions.DefaultAmp
member.summary: Gets or sets the default amplitude used during initial setup, in mA. The default is 1.0.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.WssStimulationCoreOptions.DefaultSync
member.summary: Gets or sets the default sync group used during initial setup. The default is 170.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.WssStimulationCoreOptions.DefaultRatio
member.summary: Gets or sets the default event ratio used during initial setup. The default is 8.

member.name: P:Wss.CoreModule.WssStimulationCoreOptions.DefaultIpd
member.summary: Gets or sets the default inter-phase delay used during initial setup, in microseconds. The default is 50.

member.name: T:Wss.CoreModule.WssTarget
member.summary: Addresses for targeting individual units or broadcasting to all. Values are on-wire destination IDs used by commands.

member.summary: Validates the current controller mode from model config (e.g., P or PD).

member.name: M:Wss.ModelModule.IModelParamsCore.StimWithMode(System.Int32,System.Single)
member.summary: Computes a pulse width from using the active controller mode and stimulates the resolved channel. Updates cached per-channel PW and amp internally.

example.code: model.StimWithMode("index", 0.42f); // P/PD mapping → PW → StimulateAnalog model.StimWithMode("ch3", 0.75f);

member.name: M:Wss.ModelModule.IModelParamsCore.GetModelConfigController
member.summary: Returns the model-layer configuration controller used to read and write model constants (e.g., proportional/derivative gains, offsets, and mode selection).
member.returns: The active instance.
member.remarks: Use this to query constants like PModeProportional or to persist model settings.

member.name: T:Wss.ModelModule.ModelConfig

member.summary: Initializes a model configuration backed by the JSON file at . Creates the file with defaults when missing.

member.name: T:Wss.ModelModule.ModelConfigController
member.summary: High-level controller for persistence and keyed access. Paths may be file or directory; if a directory is given, the file name modelConfig.json is used. Dotted keys address JSON nodes, e.g. "calib.mode", "calib.targets.index.threshold".

member.name: M:Wss.ModelModule.ModelConfigController.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Create or open a model config at . If is a directory, appends modelConfig.json. Ensures the file exists by saving defaults when first created.
member.param: File path like "C:\data\modelConfig.json" or directory like "C:\data\".
member.exception: When is null or whitespace.

example.code: var mc = new ModelConfigController(@"Configs\"); // uses Configs\modelConfig.json var mc2 = new ModelConfigController(@"Configs\m.json"); // uses given file directly

member.name: M:Wss.ModelModule.ModelConfigController.SaveModelJson
member.summary: Save the current in-memory model configuration to disk. Uses . Call after any Set/Update to persist changes.

member.name: M:Wss.ModelModule.ModelConfigController.LoadModelJson
member.summary: Reload the current model configuration from its existing path. Reinstantiates the underlying so disk edits are reflected in memory.

example.code: // external tool edited the JSON on disk: mc.LoadModelJson(); // refresh memory view

member.name: M:Wss.ModelModule.ModelConfigController.LoadModelJson(System.String)
member.summary: Load a model configuration from a new path. If is a directory, appends modelConfig.json. Replaces the active config instance.
member.param: File path like "..\profiles\a.json" or directory like "..\profiles\".
member.exception: When is null or whitespace.

member.exception: If is null or whitespace.

member.name: M:Wss.ModelModule.ModelConfigController.GetModelParam``1(System.String)
member.summary: Get a required typed value from a dotted key. Throws if the key is missing or the value cannot be converted to .
member.returns: Value at the key as .
member.exception: If is null or whitespace.
If the key does not exist.

member.name: M:Wss.ModelModule.ModelConfigController.TryGetModelParam``1(System.String,``0@)

example.code: if (mc.TryGetModelParam("calib.targets.thumb.offset", out float off)) { /* use off */ }

member.name: T:Wss.ModelModule.ModelParamsLayer
member.summary: Model-driven layer (P/PD) that sits on top of an existing params layer. Computes controller output from magnitudes and stimulates via the wrapped core.

member.name: M:Wss.ModelModule.ModelParamsLayer.#ctor(Wss.CalibrationModule.IStimParamsCore,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a model layer over an existing params/core implementation.
member.param: Model JSON file path or directory.
member.exception: Thrown when is null.

member.name: M:Wss.ModelModule.ModelParamsLayer.EnsureModelArrays
member.summary: Ensures model-layer working arrays exist and match the current channel count. Starts the internal stopwatch on first use and initializes per-channel state.
member.remarks: Channel count is derived from on the inner core. Arrays hold last magnitude, derivative estimate, and previous timestamps per channel.

member.name: M:Wss.ModelModule.ModelParamsLayer.VerifyStimMode
member.summary: Verifies that the active controller mode in the model config is supported.
member.remarks: Sets the private flag _validMode to true for modes "P" or "PD", false otherwise. Uses _modelCfg.GetCalibMode().

member.name: M:Wss.ModelModule.ModelParamsLayer.CalculateMagnitude(System.Int32,System.Single)
member.summary: Computes a normalized output magnitude for a 1-based channel using the current controller mode.
member.returns: Normalized output in [0,1] after applying P or PD mapping with model constants and a finite-difference derivative. Returns 0 if the channel index is out of range.
member.remarks: Updates per-channel caches: current magnitude, previous magnitude, derivative estimate, and last timestamp (seconds). PD mode uses keys: PDModeDerivative, PDModeProportional, PDModeOffset. P mode uses keys: PModeProportional, PModeOffset. Missing constants fall back to defaults used in code.

member.name: M:Wss.ModelModule.ModelParamsLayer.Clamp01(System.Single)
member.summary: Clamps a float to the inclusive range [0,1].
member.returns: 0 if < 0, 1 if > 1, otherwise .
